title: Cultural Heritage Sites
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Cultural heritage sites are places (0) of historical, cultural, or scientific significance that societies choose to preserve for future generations. These sites, which range from ancient monuments to historic city centres, provide invaluable insights into human civilisation.||UNESCO's World Heritage List includes over 1,100 sites (9) from around the world, recognising locations that have 'outstanding universal value'. Sites are selected based (10) on strict criteria, including their uniqueness, authenticity, and integrity. Some famous examples include the Pyramids of Giza, the Great Wall of China, and Machu Picchu, (11) which attract millions of tourists annually.||However, preserving heritage sites presents numerous challenges. Natural disasters, climate change, pollution, and war (12) have all taken their toll on precious monuments. Tourism itself can be a double-edged sword – (13) while it provides funding for conservation, excessive visitor numbers can damage fragile structures and disturb local communities.||Balancing preservation with public access requires careful management. Some sites have introduced visitor limits, (14) while others use virtual reality technology to allow people to experience the site without causing physical damage. Local communities should also (15) be involved in preservation decisions, as they often have deep connections to these places. (16) As our world changes rapidly, protecting cultural heritage sites becomes increasingly important for maintaining links to our shared human past.

example.text: Cultural heritage sites are places (0) historical, cultural, or scientific significance.

questions.explanation: Preposición 'from' indica el origen de los sitios incluidos.
Expresión 'based on' significa fundamentado en ciertos criterios.
Pronombre relativo que se refiere a los sitios famosos mencionados.
Presente perfecto: 'have taken' con sujeto plural.
Conjunción concesiva que introduce un contraste sobre el turismo.
Conjunción que contrasta diferentes estrategias de gestión.
Voz pasiva modal: 'should be involved' expresa recomendación.
Conjunción temporal 'As' significa 'a medida que' el mundo cambia.
